mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as market participants reprice growth expectations downward.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive policy stance
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. This mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal: each 1% decline in PAYEMS from its 12-month peak predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days due to earnings compression and recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 156,105 thousand as of May 2023
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with PAYEMS 12-month percentage change (correlation > -0.15), or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series decline simultaneously by > 2% month-over-month, the mechanism triggers a negative outlook: reduced consumer spending and housing investment compress corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% MoM AND HOUST declines > 2% MoM in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 686,573 million and HOUST at 1,401 thousand as of April 2023
regime_hint: late-cycle contraction or demand shock regime
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS and HOUST declines fail to predict negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, signaling supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reassess profit margins under constrained production capacity and elevated input costs.
failure_condition: INDPRO MoM growth < 0.5% fails to predict negative 5-day returns in > 45% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates to below 3.0% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains above 4.5%, signaling the Fed's tightening cycle is successfully cooling demand without triggering recession. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as terminal rate expectations stabilize and equity risk premiums compress.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 303.29 and FEDFUNDS at 5.06% as of May 2023
regime_hint: soft-landing scenario with disinflation and stable policy rates
failure_condition: Joint condition of CPIAUCSL YoY < 3.0% and FEDFUNDS > 4.5% fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month rolling window, signaling labor market deterioration and increased recession probability. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice earnings expectations and increase equity risk premiums.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession regime
failure_condition: UNRATE 3-month rise > 0.3pp fails to predict negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate in the most recent quarter, signaling late-cycle weakness and reduced corporate earnings growth. This always-on trend mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices terminal growth expectations and equity valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 20,246.4 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q1 2023
failure_condition: GDPC1 annualized growth < 1.5% shows no significant negative correlation with 60-day SPX returns (correlation > -0.12), or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) both rise simultaneously, with INDPRO growth > 1.0% MoM and CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 4.0%, signaling stagflation risk. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors flee equities due to margin compression and monetary policy uncertainty.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth > 1.0% AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.01 and CPIAUCSL at 303.29 as of May 2023
regime_hint: stagflation or supply-shock regime with persistent inflation
failure_condition: Joint INDPRO MoM > 1.0% and CPIAUCSL YoY > 4.0% fails to predict negative 5-day returns in > 50% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.
